meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop searching for project ideas. Learn how to use freelance pitching to build real-world apps with Antigravity and Claude Code. Perfect for intermediate devs.
content.answer_block: Stop building hypothetical apps that nobody uses. Instead, identify a niche business problem, pitch a custom AI-driven solution to a local owner, and build it for them. This approach forces you to solve real constraints, master production workflows, and creates a high-value portfolio piece that actually generates revenue.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the cursor blinking like a metronome counting down your wasted potential. You’ve mastered the tutorials, but the 'to-do list' apps and weather trackers don't move the needle on your career. The dread of 'no project ideas' isn't a lack of creativity; it's a lack of stakes.

Real engineering happens when a client is waiting on the other side of your commits. By shifting from building for yourself to freelance pitching for local businesses, you transform from a student into a consultant. This is the bridge between theory and a high-paying career.

steps.title: Identify a High-Value Niche
steps.description: Find a local business with manual data entry or repetitive communication tasks. Scope a small, high-impact AI tool that solves one specific pain point.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this business website [URL] and identify 3 manual processes that could be automated via an AI agent for a small business owner.

steps.title: Draft the Value-Based Pitch
steps.description: Create a pitch that focuses on ROI rather than code. Use Claude to craft a message that speaks the language of business owners.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a 3-sentence cold email pitch for a custom AI automation tool. Focus on time saved and ROI for a small business owner.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file pitch.txt --prompt 'draft email for business owner'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Structure
steps.description: Define the stack. Keep it lean: Next.js, Supabase, and an LLM API. Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a project structure for a Next.js application with a Supabase backend and an AI integration for [Specific Task].

steps.title: Develop with AI Pair Programming
steps.description: Use Claude Code to iterate on the features. Focus on building the core logic first—don't get bogged down in CSS yet.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write the API route logic to handle user input, process it through an LLM, and store the result in Supabase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'implement api route for ai processing'

steps.title: Deploy and Iterate
steps.description: Push to Vercel and present the demo to your client. Use their feedback to refine the product in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a deployment checklist for a production-ready Next.js app on Vercel including environment variable security.

pro_tips: Always charge a nominal fee for your first project; free work is often ignored, but a paid client provides better feedback.
Focus on the 'Time-to-Value' metric—if your tool doesn't save the user at least 30 minutes a day, rethink the scope.
Document your failures during the build; explaining how you fixed a production bug is more impressive in interviews than a perfect demo.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API endpoints; business owners value reliability over flashy UI.
Build a 'Case Study' page within your own portfolio site using the metrics you gathered from your freelance client.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is deployed, reach out to three more businesses in the same niche to scale your solution into a micro-SaaS product.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a professional. You have the tools, the strategy, and the roadmap. The gap between where you are and a senior-level portfolio is just one project away. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
